reasoning: Recent FRED data for WM1NS shows strong downward momentum, having dropped for 4 consecutive weeks from 18826.5 on July 7 to 18685.2 on July 28 (roughly 40-50 billion per week). Historical data also points to significant seasonal weakness in August. Given this immediate downward trajectory, an increase by August 10 relative to the August 3 base is very unlikely. As the forecast horizon extends to September, November, and January 2026, forecasting uncertainty grows proportionally. Since 2024, M1 has largely fluctuated in a non-trending sideways band between 18,000 and 18,800, leading longer-term forecasts to be dominated by random walk variance rather than a strong directional drift. In direct accordance with the instructions, the expanding uncertainty naturally pulls the probabilities progressively closer to 0.5.
